message:
feat(abi): port __dso_handle Itanium C++ ABI per-DSO handle (bd-u1nwl)

Adds the `__dso_handle` global symbol required by the Itanium C++
ABI. It is a `void *` defined per-DSO whose **address** serves as
the unique handle that callers (notably `__cxa_atexit(func, arg,
dso_handle)`) pass as the third argument.

Without this symbol, C++ programs linking against our libc fail
at static-init time when the compiler emits
`__cxa_atexit(..., &__dso_handle)` for static-storage destructors.

Implementation: a single no_mangle `pub static __dso_handle:
AtomicPtr<c_void>` initialized to NULL. The stored value is
conventionally NULL (static-link layout) or the symbol's own
address (shared-link layout); only the address of the symbol is
observable to callers, not the value. Using AtomicPtr keeps the
ABI shape (one pointer-sized field) compatible with the C
declaration `void *__dso_handle;` while satisfying Rust's
Send + Sync requirements.

Tests: 2 abi integration tests covering (a) symbol resolves and
two reads of `&__dso_handle` return the same pointer (proves it's
a real global, not a temporary); (b) `&__dso_handle` works as the
DSO key for `__cxa_atexit` and `__cxa_finalize` — register a
destructor with that key, run finalize once, the destructor fires
exactly once; running finalize a second time does NOT re-fire.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-di5w","title":"Track A: Implement _IO_iter_* native stream iteration over NativeStreamRegistry","description":"## Goal
Replace 4 stub _IO_iter_* functions in io_internal_abi.rs (lines 1430-1457) with real implementations that iterate over the NativeStreamRegistry.

## Current State
All 4 functions return null, making the stream list appear empty:
- _IO_iter_begin() -> null (should return first occupied slot index)
- _IO_iter_end() -> null (should return sentinel past last slot)
- _IO_iter_file(iter) -> null (should return FILE* for the slot)
- _IO_iter_next(iter) -> null (should advance to next occupied slot)

## Why This Matters
glibc atexit/fflush-all path may walk _IO_list via these iterators. If the list is empty, exit-time flushing of native streams is silently skipped by any code using glibc's _IO_iter API. Our native fflush(NULL) works via NativeStreamRegistry.flush_all(), but external code (atexit handlers in host glibc) that walks _IO_list via iterators would miss our streams.

## Design
Use slot-index-as-opaque-pointer encoding:
- _IO_iter_begin: Lock registry, find first OCCUPIED slot (index 0..255), return (index+1) cast to *mut c_void. Return null if no occupied slots. We use index+1 so that 0 (stdin at slot 0) is distinguishable from null.
- _IO_iter_end: Return (STREAM_REGISTRY_CAPACITY+1) cast to *mut c_void as sentinel. This is 257, always > any valid encoded slot.
- _IO_iter_file: Decode (iter as usize - 1) to get slot index, call registry.get(index), return &NativeFile as *mut c_void. Return null if index is invalid or slot is empty.
- _IO_iter_next: Decode current slot index from iter, scan forward for next OCCUPIED slot, return encoded index or end sentinel.

### Thread Safety
The registry is behind a Mutex. Each iterator call acquires and releases the lock independently. This means the iteration is NOT atomic — streams could be added/removed between calls. This matches glibc behavior (glibc's _IO_iter functions are also not atomic without _IO_list_all_lock held externally).

### Implementation Helpers
Add to NativeStreamRegistry:
- first_occupied() -> Option<usize>: scan slots[0..] for first SLOT_OCCUPIED
- next_occupied(after: usize) -> Option<usize>: scan slots[after+1..] for next SLOT_OCCUPIED

## Files Changed
- crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/io_internal_abi.rs: Replace 4 function bodies, add 2 helpers to NativeStreamRegistry

## Unit Tests (in tests/io_internal_native_file_test.rs)
- test iter_begin_returns_first_stream: Register 0 extra streams -> begin iterates over stdin/stdout/stderr (3 entries)
- test iter_begin_end_empty_after_unregister: Unregister all stdio, begin == end (no streams)
- test iter_file_returns_valid_native_file: iter_file on valid iterator returns non-null with correct fd
- test iter_file_on_end_returns_null: iter_file on end sentinel returns null
- test iter_next_advances_to_next_occupied: skip empty slots correctly
- test iter_next_at_end_returns_end: advancing past last stream returns end sentinel
- test iter_full_traversal_count: register 5 streams, iterate begin->next->next...->end, count matches open_count()
- test iter_survives_concurrent_unregister: unregister a stream during iteration, no crash

## e2e Test Script: scripts/check_io_iter_native.sh
Build and run a focused Rust test binary that:
1. Opens 10 files via our native fopen (if available) or memfd_create
2. Walks _IO_iter_begin -> _IO_iter_next until _IO_iter_end
3. Counts entries and verifies >= 10
4. Emits structured JSONL trace per step

## Structured Logging
Each function emits: trace_id, api_family=io_internal, symbol=_IO_iter_begin|end|file|next, slot_index, result_ptr, latency_ns

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 4 _IO_iter_* functions return real iterators over NativeStreamRegistry
- 8+ unit tests pass covering positive, negative, edge, and concurrency cases
- e2e script passes
- cargo clippy clean
- LD_PRELOAD smoke suite (58 passes) not regressed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:17:17.216902967Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:45:57.935547570Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:45:57.935252511Z","close_reason":"Implemented _IO_iter_begin/end/file/next over NativeStreamRegistry using slot-index-as-opaque-pointer encoding. Added first_occupied()/next_occupied() helpers. 8 new integration tests: begin returns first stream, end is constant sentinel, file returns valid NativeFile, file on end/null returns null, next past end stays at end, full traversal visits all streams, traversal after unregister skips freed slots. All 40 io_internal tests pass. Clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io-internal","native","stub-elimination"],"comments":[{"id":1361,"issue_id":"bd-di5w","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implementation note: NativeStreamRegistry already has get() and open_count(). Use slot index as opaque pointer (usize cast to *mut c_void). Sentinel for _IO_iter_end = STREAM_REGISTRY_CAPACITY (256). Lock registry mutex during scan. Consider exposing first_occupied()/next_occupied() helpers on NativeStreamRegistry.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:20:49Z"}]}

Add isolated harness subprocess execution for tests/conformance/fixtures/grp_ops.json, assert expected_output and host parity by mode, and fix any matrix-path regressions the new coverage exposes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T05:43:33.470596927Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T05:48:23.475094946Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T05:48:23.472922473Z","close_reason":"Added isolated harness-matrix execution for grp_ops.json and verified locally and via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-dqqh1","title":"[HIGH] rpc_abi_test parallel race: free(): invalid size SIGABRT at --test-threads>=4 (locally reproducible bd-ye0f4 sibling)","description":"## Symptom

cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test rpc_abi_test under default cargo
parallelism aborts with free(): invalid size:

    running 35 tests
    RPC: Success
    RPC: Timed out
    RPC: Unknown error
    free(): invalid size
    error: test failed, to rerun pass -p frankenlibc-abi --test rpc_abi_test
    process didn't exit successfully: .../rpc_abi_test-... (signal: 6, SIGABRT)

The 3 RPC lines come from clnt_perrno_does_not_crash, which runs
early. Abort fires AFTER those lines, meaning another parallel-
running test is the culprit, not clnt_perrno itself.

## Bisection (LOCAL — unlike bd-ye0f4 which only reproduced on rch)

    --test-threads=1: 35 passed, 0 failed
    --test-threads=2: 35 passed, 0 failed
    --test-threads=4: SIGABRT with free(): invalid size
    --test-threads=8: SIGABRT with free(): invalid size (default)

Locally reproducible on a 64-core x86_64 host at HEAD 17431c7a —
threshold is exactly at --test-threads=4. This is the first
locally-reproducible sample of the bd-ye0f4 cluster's
'free(): invalid size' signature. Previous cluster beads (bd-g2bd5,
bd-ye0f4) only reproduced under rch remote execution with the
identical glibc error.

## Why this matters

The bd-ye0f4 cluster has been cycling fix→regress since
2026-04-22 across a581f0ac, e289f838, b04d7238 (all partial TLS
dtor scoping fixes). Every round left the race live. Root cause
has been elusive because nobody could reproduce it locally — only
on rch workers with specific worker scheduling. This rpc_abi_test
sample is a deterministic local reproducer: same
'free(): invalid size' glibc detection, pinpoints the race at the
4+ thread boundary, and runs every time.

## Diagnosis candidates

clnt_perrno_does_not_crash routes through crate::unistd_abi::write.
write() fans into runtime_policy::decide (complex state machine
with potentially-racy internal globals) and runtime_policy::observe
(recording validations). Other tests in the same binary exercise
rpc_thread_createerr, rpc_thread_svc_*, rpc_thread_tls_* — thread-
local getters that allocate buffers.

The most likely explanation: one test's thread-local RPC state is
freed by another thread's teardown (thread-local cross-allocator
free), or runtime_policy has a per-thread AtomicI32 / RefCell that
gets torn-down by one test while another test is mid-decide.

## Fix direction

1. Re-run with RUST_BACKTRACE=1 MALLOC_CHECK_=3 (already tried, no
   extra info — glibc's native free() abort is silent). Next level:
   compile with RUSTFLAGS='-Zsanitizer=address' (nightly) and rerun
   the 4-thread test to get an ASan stack trace pointing at the
   freeing site and the allocating site.
2. Audit all thread-locals in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/rpc_abi.rs
   and crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/runtime_policy.rs for allocation
   Drop impls that could free pointers allocated by a different
   thread's allocator. rpc_abi.rs has SPERRNO_BUF and
   SPCREATEERR_BUF (Cell<[u8; N]>) which are inline arrays — safe.
   But __rpc_thread_createerr, __rpc_thread_svc_fdset,
   __rpc_thread_svc_max_pollfd, __rpc_thread_svc_pollfd return
   pointers into allocations — those are the likely culprits.
3. Check the 3+ rpc_thread_* tests: if they allocate in one test
   thread and the Drop fires on the next test thread (pthread-local
   rebinding), we get cross-allocator free.

## Validation

cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test rpc_abi_test -- --test-threads=2
  -> 35 passed, 0 failed.
cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test rpc_abi_test -- --test-threads=4
  -> SIGABRT, free(): invalid size.

No commit changes needed to repro. Provides the long-awaited local
sample for the bd-ye0f4 cluster.","notes":"

## ROOT-CAUSE FIX by cc-libc (2026-04-22)

Bisected by running rpc_abi_test with progressively narrower test-name filters at --test-threads=4. Convergence:

    cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test rpc_abi_test 'host2netname_' -- --test-threads=2  -> SIGABRT free(): invalid size
    cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test rpc_abi_test host2netname_creates_valid_name -- --test-threads=4  -> 1/1 pass
    cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test rpc_abi_test host2netname_null_defaults     -- --test-threads=4  -> 1/1 pass

Either test alone passes; the two together at N>=2 abort. They share no test-side state — but they BOTH allocate via host2netname() and BOTH free via libc::free().

The actual bug at crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/rpc_abi.rs:2874:

    let buf = unsafe { crate::malloc_abi::raw_alloc(name.len()) } as *mut c_char;

host2netname allocates through OUR internal arena (raw_alloc), but the test releases the buffer via libc::free (host glibc's free). In debug builds (test context, no LD_PRELOAD), libc::free binds at link time to glibc's free — which sees a chunk header from our arena, interprets it as a glibc chunk-size field, gets nonsense, and aborts with 'free(): invalid size'.

The race is just chunk-layout entropy: with 1 thread the same allocator context is reused so chunks happen to look fine; with 2+ threads the arena's per-thread chunk metadata diverges from glibc's expectations and the free hits a bad size word deterministically.

## Fix

Change host2netname to allocate via libc::malloc instead of our internal raw_alloc. POSIX defines host2netname as 'returns a buffer the caller must free()' — that means the caller's free() (glibc's in non-preload context, our interposed one in preload context) must match the alloc. libc::malloc satisfies both: in preload context it IS our interposed malloc, in non-preload context it's glibc's, and either way it pairs correctly with libc::free.

## Validation

cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test rpc_abi_test (default parallelism) -> 35 passed, 0 failed (was SIGABRT). All thread counts 1..8 now pass.

## Wider implication for the bd-ye0f4 cluster

30+ other call sites use the same `crate::malloc_abi::raw_alloc` pattern and return the buffer to the caller (inet_abi.rs, dirent_abi.rs, stdlib_abi.rs, more in rpc_abi.rs, wchar_abi.rs, unistd_abi.rs, string_abi.rs). Each is a candidate sibling failure. Every test that exercises one of those entrypoints AND frees with libc::free is a potential next bd-dqqh1. Filed as an umbrella bead for systematic audit.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-libc","created_at":"2026-04-22T22:29:08.914661512Z","created_by":"cc-libc","updated_at":"2026-04-22T22:56:51.292886Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T22:56:51.292886Z","close_reason":"Fixed: host2netname now uses libc::malloc instead of internal raw_alloc; alloc/free pair consistent with caller libc::free in both LD_PRELOAD and non-preload contexts.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["abi","free-invalid-size","full-review","locally-reproducible","race","rpc","test"]}

+{"id":"bd-u1nwl","title":"PORT: __dso_handle (Itanium C++ ABI per-DSO handle)","description":"Port the Itanium C++ ABI `__dso_handle` global symbol. It is a `void *` defined per-DSO that callers (notably `__cxa_atexit(func, arg, dso_handle)`) use as a unique handle identifying the dynamic shared object. Standard convention: define it as a pointer that holds its own address (so the address is unique per-DSO and stable across linkage). Without it, C++ programs linking against our libc fail at static-init time when the compiler emits `__cxa_atexit(..., &__dso_handle)`. Implementation: single line — `pub static __dso_handle: ... = ...` exported as a no_mangle symbol. Tests verify the symbol resolves and __cxa_atexit accepts &__dso_handle as the dso_handle argument.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T10:21:22.453995752Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T10:21:22.453995752Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Add ABI regression coverage so nonzero flags preserve that observed privilege-first result.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:13:38.057709475Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T22:26:05.250252843Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T22:26:05.248052037Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
